content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. For any pair of integers (l,r)(l, r)(l,r) such that 1≤l≤r≤N1 \le l \le r \le N1≤l≤r≤N, let's define OR(l,r)\mathrm{OR}(l, r)OR(l,r) as Al∨Al+1∨…∨ArA_l \lor A_{l+1} \lor \ldots \lor A_rAl​∨Al+1​∨…∨Ar​. Here, ∨\lor∨ is the bitwise OR operator.

In total, there are N(N+1)2\frac{N(N+1)}{2}2N(N+1)​ possible pairs (l,r)(l, r)(l,r), i.e. N(N+1)2\frac{N(N+1)}{2}2N(N+1)​ possible values of OR(l,r)\mathrm{OR}(l, r)OR(l,r). Determine if all these values are pairwise distinct.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 3·10^5
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains an integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 10^5). Second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN (0 ≤ Ai ≤ 10^18).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if all values OR(l, r) for 1 ≤ l ≤ r ≤ N are pairwise distinct, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: subarrays are [1], [2], [1,2] → OR values 1,2,3 are distinct → YES.\nTest case 2: subarrays produce OR values 1,2,7,3,7,7 → duplicate 7 → NO.
Only one subarray exists, its OR is 0, so all values are distinct.
Every subarray OR equals 5, many duplicates → NO.

solution.approach: For each position i we keep the set of distinct OR values of all subarrays that end at i. When we move to i+1 we can obtain the new set by taking A[i+1] alone and OR‑ing A[i+1] with every value from the previous set. Because OR is monotone, the size of this set never exceeds the number of bits (≤60 for 10^18), so the total work per element is O(60). All generated values are inserted into a global hash set; if a value is already present we immediately know the answer is NO. If we finish the array without a clash the answer is YES.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N·B) per test case, where B ≤ 60 (number of bits of Ai). Overall O(total N·60).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N·B) for the global hash set (at most N·60 distinct values) plus O(B) for the current list of ORs.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[]args)throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());while(T-- >0){int N=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());long[]A=new long[N];StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());for(int i=0;i<N;i++)A[i]=Long.parseUnsignedLong(st.nextToken());HashSet<Long>seen=new HashSet<>((int)(N*60.0));ArrayList<Long>cur=new ArrayList<>();boolean ok=true;for(int i=0;i<N && ok;i++){ArrayList<Long>nxt=new ArrayList<>();nxt.add(A[i]);for(Long x:cur){long v=x|A[i];if(!nxt.contains(v))nxt.add(v);}cur=nxt;for(Long v:cur){if(!seen.add(v)){ok=false;break;}}}sb.append(ok?"YES":"NO").append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
code.Python3:
import sys
def solve():
    data=sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    if not data:return
    it=iter(data)
    T=int(next(it))
    out=[]
    for _ in range(T):
        N=int(next(it))
        A=[int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        seen=set()
        cur=[]
        ok=True
        for a in A:
            nxt=[a]
            for x in cur:
                nxt.append(x|a)
            uniq=[]
            local=set()
            for v in nxt:
                if v not in local:
                    local.add(v)
                    uniq.append(v)
            cur=uniq
            for v in cur:
                if v in seen:
                    ok=False
                    break
                seen.add(v)
            if not ok:
                break
        out.append('YES' if ok else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))
if __name__=="__main__":
    solve()
